Input: File F uploaded by user
Output: Parsed text chunks and extracted images

2: if (format = DOC or PPT) then

4: end if

5: Extract raw text using file-type-specific parser

6: Extract embedded images (if any)

7: Store metadata in SQLite

ecc9439f-38ef-46f8-b6c2-0172079ee012.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

86300502-e032-4f02-8857-d1ec8ac4859b.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

d43ecc9b-8917-4872-a82f-45ec2413a84e.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

3f2f8ded-7d4c-43c0-912e-1b6d6254c4a7.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

3b9bf5fb-9433-4229-9661-a002873c03ae.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

d6fa532f-199a-495b-b3b6-d0c8d4552a49.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

e5cc0bfc-8f7d-45aa-b08e-dc9e274dab9c.response:
Functional Requirements (37 items (Avg Quality: 65/100)):
- ✅ System shall be constructed bas ed on the C++ Virtual Studio to ensure that only the internal staff shall be able to access the data (see F igure 5 ).
- ⚠️  ️ System shall be able to solve the oversupply problems which are due to inaccurate information and the tende ncy to increase inventory levels [6].
- ⚠️  ️ Management system shall be able to solve the oversupply problems which are due to inaccurate information and the tende ncy to increase inventory levels [6].
- ⚠️  ️ In addition, an inventory management system shall be able to solve the oversupply problems which are due to inaccurate information and the tende ncy to increase inventory levels [6].
- ⚠️  ️ System shall be able to solve the oversupply problems which are due to inaccurate information and the i.
- ⚠️  ️ This project aims to develop an Enterprise I nformation System (EIS) which shall be able to improve retail companies ’ daily activity coordination and inventory management.
- ⚠️  ️ Transaction Processing Systems and Enterprise Applications shall be co nstructed for data transfer between each branch and the central data warehouse of retail organizations.
- ⚠️  ️ It is hoped that retail companies ’ staff shall find this system to be efficient and serviceable for checking stock levels and r equest ing product t ransfers.
- ⚠️  ️ Flow chart of searching for sales records Finally, a login system shall be constructed bas ed on the C++ Virtual Studio to ensure that only the internal staff shall be able to access the data (see F igure 5 ).
- ⚠️  ️ SQL, C++, HTML, and JavaScript codes shall be used to implement the logic flow of sales record.
- ⚠️  ️ The Relational Model Terminology shall be applied in this design and used to build the Relational Model Database.
- ⚠️  ️ Inventory management TPS for each independent branch of a retail company MySQL shall be used to design the database for data management and storage.
- ⚠️  ️ Processing unit which shall be able to be utilized in various retail companies for their daily activities coordination including: 1) Daily Inventory Management: Sharing inventory levels of all branches with employees to respond to customer orders more efficiently.
- System shall be able to effective ly achieve the integrati on of heterogeneous data, which further realizes the potential of t he information service for company producti vity, sales, and management.
- This may cause a situation in which customers shall wait for a long time while employees manually check stock shelves for products or find information on the computer and call another branch for a single item [2].
- As pointed out by Ma and Wang [3], an enterprise information management and decision support system shall be able to effective ly achieve the integrati on of heterogeneous data, which further realizes the potential of t he information service for company producti vity, sales, and management.

e5cc0bfc-8f7d-45aa-b08e-dc9e274dab9c.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

d29f0a1e-4226-42d6-9d29-352f5bc8aab2.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

7b514dea-82a5-40ad-a429-13133691a3f5.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

ce3d9d52-6364-4d60-a039-6048d548210e.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

10c695a9-2930-4f0c-9e83-f0c1d9c7c538.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

4b3cae6a-6b6f-42ee-857b-3acb8c80416f.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

dfdcebc9-74f9-4fec-8d80-c13d48992980.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

4f9516bf-a783-405e-a53d-e5a40e7aa33f.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

90186ea6-3234-42a6-ac6e-042a2f3d955e.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

c34cfe7e-4277-4dc1-9fbc-a9934c14125e.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

6f0ac3ea-be1f-4bd4-9e00-76d13018a70b.response:
Functional Requirements (55 items (Avg Quality: 68/100)):
- ✅ The system architecture shall allow the using of cluster hardware and support multi-processor systems.
- ✅ Priority 1 Source Risk C • High (H) – shall impact the main functionality of the system.

6f0ac3ea-be1f-4bd4-9e00-76d13018a70b.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

ebeb5890-db83-4aae-b95f-b2549b4eaf91.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

1615f271-adbd-4b6d-bd17-cfd0363e6035.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

2ddde7e6-5f5a-4683-b53a-95c27ed7719c.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

84b25738-b829-490e-be0f-c39b2f660186.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

359162ea-49ff-42e6-ad5a-65a33fe428b9.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

5a73798a-eb20-4df7-b309-26303400c626.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

26f8f38e-ecc6-4109-bd2a-0b9ece028ab3.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

05c81940-4815-4f33-9b4e-3333e9b3cb82.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

(2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

75338ef0-fef1-4c84-96a8-1501c2083024.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

87c8182b-7852-4de1-a22e-bd6230dcdc4a.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

4215c6b4-7fe6-4d1f-9fac-4864e0aed24c.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

d40a0b3c-1eb5-42e8-a5d6-2027d798bf25.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

440d6c95-b58e-40f2-870f-fb96de27c853.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

0f04a948-ad4e-4b66-a63e-63a90ed10564.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

102de152-5958-4eef-98db-656a98fccf81.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

1c26eb8a-5f52-4417-8e2c-5d8ffd942c18.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.

22f64e57-f46a-46e7-afe3-7f81ba6ca7fe.requirement_classification_basis: Classification basis (high level): (1) User / story phrasing ('As a …', 'I want …', 'so that') → user requirements. (2) Non-functional: quality attributes or measurable constraints — performance, latency, security, encryption, authentication, reliability, availability, scalability, usability, maintainability, portability, compatibility, or patterns like 'within N ms', uptime %, RPS, concurrent users — but not when the sentence is clearly about a business action (payment, booking, API call, calculation, etc.); those stay functional. (3) Business: policy, regulation, compliance, stakeholder, KPI-style wording. (4) Otherwise default to functional (system behaviour / capability). Extractor section headings also influence grouping before this refinement.
